chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Welcome to Manufacturing Processes, Machining and Machine Tools lectures by Professor Joy

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: G Tagosh.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is the eleventh lecture of a series of lectures.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This lecture will be on Slaughter and Planar Machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: He will be discussing functioning of a slaughter machine, comparison of shaper and slaughter,

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: specification of slaughter machine, operations that can be performed on a slaughter machine,

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: functioning of a planar machine, types of planar machines, components of a planar machine,

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: planar size, work holding, planar drive mechanism, planar feed mechanism, comparison of shapers

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: and planars and planar operations.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So, do not miss and watch the full video.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: For the next video in the lecture series, please click the suggested video link above.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: You may also click on the playlist at end of the video to access my videos.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Please subscribe this channel and yes, do not forget to like and share.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Also click on the bell icon to receive instant information of new videos uploaded.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Thank you.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Hello friends, welcome to this lecture.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: In this lecture, we will be discussing the slaughter machine and the planar machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So let us start.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: A slaughter machine just like a shaper machine works just like a shaper machine and does

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: job similar which is done on a shaper machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And therefore it is also referred to as a vertical shaper.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Slaughter machine was invented much before shaper machine was invented.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: It was invented by a person called Brunel in the year 1800.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So the major differences between a shaper and a slaughter is that in a slaughter machine,

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: we have a rotary table and because we have a rotary table, the rotary field can be given.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So a circular surface can be machined compared to that of a shaper machine which is not possible

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: in a shaper machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And the ram here travels in a vertical plane.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So the ram moves up and down.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So downward stroke is the cutting stroke and upward stroke is the return stroke.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And like shaper, the upward stroke has to return fast to reduce the non-productive time

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And therefore here also we use a quick return mechanism.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: It can be a quick return mechanism or it can be a crank and slaughter link mechanism.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So I will show you the picture in the next slide.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And shaper machine unlike slaughter machine can cut internal surfaces also provided the

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: tool and the hole is enough to accommodate the tool to enter the workpiece.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: It is used for cutting flat surfaces, particles, inclined etc. etc.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: It is also used for cutting cubase and making slots, both internal and external surfaces.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So these are some of the differences between shaper and slaughter.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is a slaughter machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: As you can see this is a circular table and we can give a circular feed.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is the ram which reciprocates and it also uses during the downward stroke it cuts

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: and during upward stroke it does not cut.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now since the weight is coming down there is a counterbalance added to the slaughter

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: machine which is not there in a shaper machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: I will show you a picture of a slaughter which has this is the counterweight.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this ram comes down along with the tool and this is a circular table here.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So some of the differences between shaper and slaughter are that shaper is horizontal,

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: slaughter is ram travels vertical in a slaughter.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Shaper has a rectangular type of table whereas there is circular table in case of a slaughter

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: and because there is circular table circular feed can be given and circular surfaces can

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: be machined.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And because of the nature of the working of the ram here moving up down so here internal

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: surfaces can be machined which is not possible in a shaper machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: In a slaughter machine there is counterweight to balance the weight moving down of the ram.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So these are difference between a shaper and a slaughter.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is the quick return mechanism you can see this is the connecting rod sorry this

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: is slotted link and this is the crank pin this is the block fitted in the slot of the

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is the lock around and this is the radius slide this is the pull gear this is the pinion

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: gear.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: The size of a slaughter is specified with the maximum length of stroke, the height between

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: the table and the tool head, the maximum longitudinal feed, the maximum transverse feed, the diameter

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: of the table these are the things that is normally specified a slaughter machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: What are the operations that can be performed in a slaughter machine is that it can machine

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: flat surfaces external, internal, vertical etc. and it can machine circular surfaces

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: also using rotary table.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: It can machine slots, skewage groups.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: These are the some of the operations that can be performed on a slaughter machines.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: With this we move on to the planer machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Planer also is used to make surfaces flat so it generates flat surfaces or machines

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: flat surfaces both vertical and horizontal it may be inclined also but there are some

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: differences between the working of a sepa machine and a planer machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Planer machine is a production type machine intended for accurate machining of surfaces

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: on large surface.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So some of the typical application of sepa machine may be machining of machine bed columns,

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: diesel engine blocks, bending plates etc.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this typical diagram shows a double housing planer.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: We will see what is a double housing planer.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is double housing planer installed in our shop.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is the table which is located on this bed and this is the cross rail, this is the

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: rail head, this is the housing, this is the housing rod, this is trip dog, this is trip

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: dog in lever, reversing lever, this is a table, these are T-slots, there is T-slots, in this

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: T-slots these are two trip dogs that can be fitted.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this distance between these two trip dog determines the length of stroke.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So you can change it according to your requirement.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: You can increase it or you can decrease it as is required.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So what are the different types of planer?

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: You can have double housing planer that means there are two housing on both sides of the

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: bed.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And on this type of planer can support and use different types of tool heads and multiple

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: cuts can be taken.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So open side planer.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: In open sided planer there is only one housing.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this allows for larger breadth of the workpiece that can be accommodated in the

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: planer.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And pit planer, this is particularly for large length workpieces.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Here the table does not reciprocate rather the rail, there is a rail which reciprocates

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: over the workpiece.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And divided table, so this is particularly a high production machine where there are

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: two tables.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So both table can move independently and here in one of the table the workpiece is being

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: machine and in other table the workpiece is mounted.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So once mounting is over and other is over, they can interchange.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this saves time.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So actually work holding in a planer is a time taking process because you have to tightly

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: and rigidly hold the workpiece on the planer because the table carrying the work reciprocates.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So if there is not properly held then accidents may happen.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So work holding is very very tedious in planer machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this is double housing planer.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: You can see this is the bed, this is the table, these are the housings, housings on both sides

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: so it is called a double housing planer and there are rail heads, these are called rail

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: heads and these are called side heads.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So four tools are there and these four tools can simultaneously take cuts.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And here this table, the size of the bed is almost twice the size of the table or the

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: length of the table rather.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: The length of the bed is almost twice the length of the table because the table reciprocates

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: on the bed.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And there are trip dogs, this is trip dogs and this is reversing lever.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So at the end of the stroke it will strike and the lever will change the belts and the

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: direction of movement of the table will change.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So these are trip dogs.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this is a feed mechanism, this is a feed disc which drives this thing, this screw and

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: this will move according to the requirement.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Okay there is a rack here number 20, please identify this table rack.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So the mechanism is ultimately fitted to this.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: The rotator mechanism we are using that transmits the motion to this rack and this rack causes

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: the table to reciprocate.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: The rotary motion is converted into linear motion by rack and pinion arrangement.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this rack converts the rotary motion into a linear motion.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And how the quick return mechanism is achieved, we will see how the quick return mechanism

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: is achieved.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: These are the major components of a planer machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Planer size, planer size is specified larger rectangular solid that can be reciprocated

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: under the tool.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So it varies slightly from double housing planer to open sided planer depending on what

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: we are specifying.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So you can go through it.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Work holding, work holding is very, very important in a planer machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And in a planer we do not use vices rather we directly mount the workpiece on the table.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: There are T-slots provided on the table and the workpieces are held rigidly and tightly

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: so that it should not move during the reciprocations.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: You have to understand that the large size workpiece are held on the table and are reciprocated.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So the momentum is quite high.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So if it is not rigidly held, accidents may happen.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So work holding is very, very important in planer machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: With this we move on to the planer drive mechanism.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So again just like how I teach mechanisms, here I will explain the walking, construction

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: first and then I will explain the walking.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So as I point out the components, you try to identify the components in the figure.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So note that there is a loose pulley 1 and tight pulley 2.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Tight pulley 2 means on the shaft 2, on the shaft 3, 2 is mounted.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So if 2 rotates, 3 rotates or if 3 rotates, 2 rotates.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And if 3 rotates or 2 rotates, it does not have any impact on 1.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And on the same shaft 3, there are other 2 pulleys.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Pulley 4 and pulley 5 are also mounted.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now and the pulley 4, smaller pulley 4, 4 and 5 are different in diameter and pulley

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: 4 is small and pulley 5 is larger.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is by design.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is not by chance.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So it is intentionally made smaller and pulley 4 matches with another pulley 11 which is

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: fixed here, which is a fixed pulley on shaft 22.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: On shaft 22, 11 is fixed.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So power is transmitted from pulley 4 to pulley 11 using a cross belt drive, using

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: a cross belt drive in the current position.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And pulley 5 is fixed on shaft 3, is connected to a pulley 9 which is loose, running loose

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: on shaft 22, which is running loose on shaft 22.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: There are other 2 pulleys in this shaft 22.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Pulley number 10 which is a fixed pulley and pulley number 12 which is a loose pulley.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Okay, now 22, the shaft 22 is connected to the rack 15.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: In the previous figure I have shown you, in the construction of planer, there is a rack

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: below the bed.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This 15 is connected to 14 here.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And it is a gear train, so the motion of this shaft 22 is ultimately transmitted to

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: 14 and 14 drives the table using the pinion, so rack 15.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So linear rotary motion of the 14 is converted to linear motion of the rack 15 at the table,

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So there is a trip dog 18 and a lever, that is, belt shifting lever 17.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this is the components.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now let us see the working.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So here, currently, let us say the power from the motor is transmitted to the shaft 2, which

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: is fixed on the shaft 3.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now you can shift it here and you can stop the machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So once the machine, when the belt is in 2, the machine is running.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So you can stop and start the machine by shifting the belt.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now let us say the belt is in 2 and shaft 3 is rotating.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So when shaft 3 is rotating, 4 and 5 are rotating.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now when 4 is rotating, the power is transmitted to the shaft 11, so shaft, sorry, yes, pulley

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: 11 and the pulley 11 rotates, which in turn rotates the shaft 22 and it drives the rack

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: 15 ultimately.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: The shaft, it is, forward stroke is taking place.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So forward stroke is taking place.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: At the end of the forward stroke, at the end of the forward stroke, this trip dog is set

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: and it strikes the belt shifting lever.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Once it strikes the belt shifting lever, this is the belt shifting lever, so it will shift

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: the belt.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So the belt will now, at the end of the stroke, forward stroke, the belt will shift from fast

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: pulley 11 to loose pulley 12 and from loose pulley 9 to fast pulley 10.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now it is a cross belt drive and it is an open belt drive.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Obviously the direction of rotation will be different.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now the power will not be transmitted from 4, rather the power will be transmitted from

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: 5 to the fast pulley 10 and it will move in a reverse direction.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So return stroke will take place.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this is how the planar drive mechanism actually works.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now how this is a quick return mechanism.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now during the forward stroke, you want maximum power and less speed.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So therefore you are using a cross belt drive because the contact area between the belt

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: and the pulley is maximum.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So maximum power is transmitted and less speed.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Whereas in a, and see here from small pulley to large pulley, so less speed.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Whereas in a from large pulley to small pulley, the speed is high and it is open belt drive.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So here less power and more speed.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So during the return stroke, it returns faster.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this is how the quick return mechanism works in this type of mechanism.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So it is not necessary, this is one of the mechanism of a planar.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: It is not necessary that all planars will have this mechanism.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is one of the mechanism that is implemented on certain types of planars.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: There are other mechanisms also which can be implemented in a planar.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Okay the feed mechanism.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Okay.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now one more thing here I want you to know that as this is rotating, there is a number

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: 16 which is called the feed disc.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Number 16 is called the feed disc.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So what is this?

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is very important.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Number 16 which is connected to shaft 23.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is shaft 23.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now let us come back to this figure.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Come back to this figure.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So here this is shaft 23.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: That is shaft, it is indicated here by 5.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: That shaft 23 is shaft 5 here.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now here the power for feed mechanism is transmitted by a flexible coupling mechanism.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now this flexible coupling mechanism is achieved by a flange.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: There is a flange which is inserted into a rotary disc, circular hole.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: In this disc is inserted, this flange is inserted and there is a rubber padding here.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So what happens during one half of the rotation, it transmits the power and drives the cross

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: feed screw and in the other half, during the return stroke, part of the return stroke,

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: it drives the cross feed screw and the rest of the time it remains inactive and does not

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: transmit any power.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Rather it slips over the leather padding.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So here you can see the flange 4 is connected to shaft 23 in the previous slide.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Here it is 5.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: It is connected by 2 bolts here, number 4, number 2 and 16 is this block 16.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is the feed disc which I have shown you in the previous diagram.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is the feed disc, same disc is shown here and on top of this, there is a leather

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: side on which this block is mounted.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Block 1 is mounted.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This block 1 is connected to a connecting rod 15.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: It is connected to a connecting rod 15 which is connected to a rack 14 and rack 14 meshes

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: with another shaft, another gear which is gear number.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is the gear on which it is meshes.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This gear, it meshes, rack meshes with this gear.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: On this gear, it is mounted on a shaft 12.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: On this same shaft, gear 9 is mounted and gear 10 is mounted.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: 10 is running loose on this shaft and gear 9 is fixed.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So if shaft 12 rotates, gear 9 rotates and it does not transmit motion to the gear 10.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So in this gear 10, there is a double sided pawl is fitted.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is called double pawl.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: You can insert this side or this side in the gear teeth 9.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: In any of the gear teeth 9.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now let us see what happens.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is the forward stroke.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: During the forward stroke, it will rotate in this direction.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So the power from here, the power is being transmitted, so this will also rotate.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: As this rotates by friction between the pad and this flange, the power will be transmitted

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: to the disc and this will rotate here in this direction.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So as it comes down, now here as it comes down, the connecting rod will come down, so

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: this rack will come down.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And we will fit this hand, let us say here, so this will rotate in.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: If this comes down, so this will rotate in this direction.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: And this hand is fixed here.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: The left hand is slid inside this here.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So when it comes down, this slides and no movement is transferred to the gear 11.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: However, and the rest of the time, it is stopped by, after a period of rotation, it is stopped

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: by a stopper 7, fixed pin 7.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Now at the end of the return stroke, forward stroke, it returns.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So the direction changes.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: When the direction changes, it returns in reverse direction.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: In reverse direction, the crank, it will move up and this rack will move up.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: When it moves up, the gear 9 will give a positive motion to the gear 11 through the

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: pawl 10.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: As a result, this shaft 4 will move because it masses and cross feed will be given.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this is how it works.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: The planar feed mechanism works in a planar.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this is one of the mechanisms.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Not necessary that all planar machines will be using this feed mechanism.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This is one of the feed mechanism which can be implemented in a planar.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So comparison of separ and planar.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: The planar is specially adapted for large work.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Separ can do only small work.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Planar, the work is moved against the stationary tool.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: On separ, the tool moves across the work which is stationary.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: On planar, tool is fed to the work and on separ, the work is fed to the tool.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: The drive on the planar is either by gears or by hydraulic machines.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: The separ ramp can also be driven in this manner.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: But many a times, a quick return mechanism is used.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Most planar deforms of separ is that they approach a more constant cutting velocity.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: In planar, the tool is rigidly supported and the work moves on precision guideways.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: This gives much more accuracy in comparison with separ where there is a cantilever thing

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: happens and there is overhanging happens.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So accuracy is compromised and surface finish is compromised.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So multiple tool can cut in a planar which is not possible in a separ machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: In planar, work setting requires skill and time whereas separ work setting is very very

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: easy.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Tool used in planar outpost will be much more robust because the work we are using is larger.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Of course, the planar machine is much heavier and costlier than separ machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So these are the some of the comparison between separ and planar which is very often asked

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: in the examination.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So you can write these comparisons and what are the different operations that can be performed

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: on a planar machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: You can machine horizontals just like separ.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: All these operations that can be performed in separ machine can also be performed on

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: a planar machine but the accuracy is high and the work piece are much larger compared

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: to that what we do in separ machine.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: So this is all about slotter and planar.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: I hope you have liked this lecture.

chunks.title: Manufacturing Proces
chunks.text: Thank you for watching.

text: Welcome to Manufacturing Processes, Machining and Machine Tools lectures by Professor Joy G Tagosh. This is the eleventh lecture of a series of lectures. This lecture will be on Slaughter and Planar Machine. He will be discussing functioning of a slaughter machine, comparison of shaper and slaughter, specification of slaughter machine, operations that can be performed on a slaughter machine, functioning of a planar machine, types of planar machines, components of a planar machine, planar size, work holding, planar drive mechanism, planar feed mechanism, comparison of shapers and planars and planar operations. So, do not miss and watch the full video. For the next video in the lecture series, please click the suggested video link above. You may also click on the playlist at end of the video to access my videos. Please subscribe this channel and yes, do not forget to like and share. Also click on the bell icon to receive instant information of new videos uploaded. Thank you. Hello friends, welcome to this lecture. In this lecture, we will be discussing the slaughter machine and the planar machine. So let us start. A slaughter machine just like a shaper machine works just like a shaper machine and does job similar which is done on a shaper machine. And therefore it is also referred to as a vertical shaper. Slaughter machine was invented much before shaper machine was invented. It was invented by a person called Brunel in the year 1800. So the major differences between a shaper and a slaughter is that in a slaughter machine, we have a rotary table and because we have a rotary table, the rotary field can be given. So a circular surface can be machined compared to that of a shaper machine which is not possible in a shaper machine. And the ram here travels in a vertical plane. So the ram moves up and down. So downward stroke is the cutting stroke and upward stroke is the return stroke. And like shaper, the upward stroke has to return fast to reduce the non-productive time And therefore here also we use a quick return mechanism. It can be a quick return mechanism or it can be a crank and slaughter link mechanism. So I will show you the picture in the next slide. And shaper machine unlike slaughter machine can cut internal surfaces also provided the tool and the hole is enough to accommodate the tool to enter the workpiece. It is used for cutting flat surfaces, particles, inclined etc. etc. It is also used for cutting cubase and making slots, both internal and external surfaces. So these are some of the differences between shaper and slaughter. This is a slaughter machine. As you can see this is a circular table and we can give a circular feed. This is the ram which reciprocates and it also uses during the downward stroke it cuts and during upward stroke it does not cut. Now since the weight is coming down there is a counterbalance added to the slaughter machine which is not there in a shaper machine. I will show you a picture of a slaughter which has this is the counterweight. So this ram comes down along with the tool and this is a circular table here. So some of the differences between shaper and slaughter are that shaper is horizontal, slaughter is ram travels vertical in a slaughter. Shaper has a rectangular type of table whereas there is circular table in case of a slaughter and because there is circular table circular feed can be given and circular surfaces can be machined. And because of the nature of the working of the ram here moving up down so here internal surfaces can be machined which is not possible in a shaper machine. In a slaughter machine there is counterweight to balance the weight moving down of the ram. So these are difference between a shaper and a slaughter. This is the quick return mechanism you can see this is the connecting rod sorry this is slotted link and this is the crank pin this is the block fitted in the slot of the machine. This is the lock around and this is the radius slide this is the pull gear this is the pinion gear. The size of a slaughter is specified with the maximum length of stroke, the height between the table and the tool head, the maximum longitudinal feed, the maximum transverse feed, the diameter of the table these are the things that is normally specified a slaughter machine. What are the operations that can be performed in a slaughter machine is that it can machine flat surfaces external, internal, vertical etc. and it can machine circular surfaces also using rotary table. It can machine slots, skewage groups. These are the some of the operations that can be performed on a slaughter machines. With this we move on to the planer machine. Planer also is used to make surfaces flat so it generates flat surfaces or machines flat surfaces both vertical and horizontal it may be inclined also but there are some differences between the working of a sepa machine and a planer machine. Planer machine is a production type machine intended for accurate machining of surfaces on large surface. So some of the typical application of sepa machine may be machining of machine bed columns, diesel engine blocks, bending plates etc. So this typical diagram shows a double housing planer. We will see what is a double housing planer. This is double housing planer installed in our shop. This is the table which is located on this bed and this is the cross rail, this is the rail head, this is the housing, this is the housing rod, this is trip dog, this is trip dog in lever, reversing lever, this is a table, these are T-slots, there is T-slots, in this T-slots these are two trip dogs that can be fitted. So this distance between these two trip dog determines the length of stroke. So you can change it according to your requirement. You can increase it or you can decrease it as is required. So what are the different types of planer? You can have double housing planer that means there are two housing on both sides of the bed. And on this type of planer can support and use different types of tool heads and multiple cuts can be taken. So open side planer. In open sided planer there is only one housing. So this allows for larger breadth of the workpiece that can be accommodated in the planer. And pit planer, this is particularly for large length workpieces. Here the table does not reciprocate rather the rail, there is a rail which reciprocates over the workpiece. And divided table, so this is particularly a high production machine where there are two tables. So both table can move independently and here in one of the table the workpiece is being machine and in other table the workpiece is mounted. So once mounting is over and other is over, they can interchange. So this saves time. So actually work holding in a planer is a time taking process because you have to tightly and rigidly hold the workpiece on the planer because the table carrying the work reciprocates. So if there is not properly held then accidents may happen. So work holding is very very tedious in planer machine. So this is double housing planer. You can see this is the bed, this is the table, these are the housings, housings on both sides so it is called a double housing planer and there are rail heads, these are called rail heads and these are called side heads. So four tools are there and these four tools can simultaneously take cuts. And here this table, the size of the bed is almost twice the size of the table or the length of the table rather. The length of the bed is almost twice the length of the table because the table reciprocates on the bed. And there are trip dogs, this is trip dogs and this is reversing lever. So at the end of the stroke it will strike and the lever will change the belts and the direction of movement of the table will change. So these are trip dogs. So this is a feed mechanism, this is a feed disc which drives this thing, this screw and this will move according to the requirement. Okay there is a rack here number 20, please identify this table rack. So the mechanism is ultimately fitted to this. The rotator mechanism we are using that transmits the motion to this rack and this rack causes the table to reciprocate. The rotary motion is converted into linear motion by rack and pinion arrangement. So this rack converts the rotary motion into a linear motion. And how the quick return mechanism is achieved, we will see how the quick return mechanism is achieved. These are the major components of a planer machine. Planer size, planer size is specified larger rectangular solid that can be reciprocated under the tool. So it varies slightly from double housing planer to open sided planer depending on what we are specifying. So you can go through it. Work holding, work holding is very, very important in a planer machine. And in a planer we do not use vices rather we directly mount the workpiece on the table. There are T-slots provided on the table and the workpieces are held rigidly and tightly so that it should not move during the reciprocations. You have to understand that the large size workpiece are held on the table and are reciprocated. So the momentum is quite high. So if it is not rigidly held, accidents may happen. So work holding is very, very important in planer machine. With this we move on to the planer drive mechanism. So again just like how I teach mechanisms, here I will explain the walking, construction first and then I will explain the walking. So as I point out the components, you try to identify the components in the figure. So note that there is a loose pulley 1 and tight pulley 2. Tight pulley 2 means on the shaft 2, on the shaft 3, 2 is mounted. So if 2 rotates, 3 rotates or if 3 rotates, 2 rotates. And if 3 rotates or 2 rotates, it does not have any impact on 1. And on the same shaft 3, there are other 2 pulleys. Pulley 4 and pulley 5 are also mounted. Now and the pulley 4, smaller pulley 4, 4 and 5 are different in diameter and pulley 4 is small and pulley 5 is larger. This is by design. This is not by chance. So it is intentionally made smaller and pulley 4 matches with another pulley 11 which is fixed here, which is a fixed pulley on shaft 22. On shaft 22, 11 is fixed. So power is transmitted from pulley 4 to pulley 11 using a cross belt drive, using a cross belt drive in the current position. And pulley 5 is fixed on shaft 3, is connected to a pulley 9 which is loose, running loose on shaft 22, which is running loose on shaft 22. There are other 2 pulleys in this shaft 22. Pulley number 10 which is a fixed pulley and pulley number 12 which is a loose pulley. Okay, now 22, the shaft 22 is connected to the rack 15. In the previous figure I have shown you, in the construction of planer, there is a rack below the bed. This 15 is connected to 14 here. And it is a gear train, so the motion of this shaft 22 is ultimately transmitted to 14 and 14 drives the table using the pinion, so rack 15. So linear rotary motion of the 14 is converted to linear motion of the rack 15 at the table, 19. So there is a trip dog 18 and a lever, that is, belt shifting lever 17. So this is the components. Now let us see the working. So here, currently, let us say the power from the motor is transmitted to the shaft 2, which is fixed on the shaft 3. Now you can shift it here and you can stop the machine. So once the machine, when the belt is in 2, the machine is running. So you can stop and start the machine by shifting the belt. Now let us say the belt is in 2 and shaft 3 is rotating. So when shaft 3 is rotating, 4 and 5 are rotating. Now when 4 is rotating, the power is transmitted to the shaft 11, so shaft, sorry, yes, pulley 11 and the pulley 11 rotates, which in turn rotates the shaft 22 and it drives the rack 15 ultimately. The shaft, it is, forward stroke is taking place. So forward stroke is taking place. At the end of the forward stroke, at the end of the forward stroke, this trip dog is set and it strikes the belt shifting lever. Once it strikes the belt shifting lever, this is the belt shifting lever, so it will shift the belt. So the belt will now, at the end of the stroke, forward stroke, the belt will shift from fast pulley 11 to loose pulley 12 and from loose pulley 9 to fast pulley 10. Now it is a cross belt drive and it is an open belt drive. Obviously the direction of rotation will be different. Now the power will not be transmitted from 4, rather the power will be transmitted from 5 to the fast pulley 10 and it will move in a reverse direction. So return stroke will take place. So this is how the planar drive mechanism actually works. Now how this is a quick return mechanism. Now during the forward stroke, you want maximum power and less speed. So therefore you are using a cross belt drive because the contact area between the belt and the pulley is maximum. So maximum power is transmitted and less speed. Whereas in a, and see here from small pulley to large pulley, so less speed. Whereas in a from large pulley to small pulley, the speed is high and it is open belt drive. So here less power and more speed. So during the return stroke, it returns faster. So this is how the quick return mechanism works in this type of mechanism. So it is not necessary, this is one of the mechanism of a planar. It is not necessary that all planars will have this mechanism. This is one of the mechanism that is implemented on certain types of planars. There are other mechanisms also which can be implemented in a planar. Okay the feed mechanism. Okay. Now one more thing here I want you to know that as this is rotating, there is a number 16 which is called the feed disc. Number 16 is called the feed disc. So what is this? This is very important. Number 16 which is connected to shaft 23. This is shaft 23. Now let us come back to this figure. Come back to this figure. So here this is shaft 23. That is shaft, it is indicated here by 5. That shaft 23 is shaft 5 here. Now here the power for feed mechanism is transmitted by a flexible coupling mechanism. Now this flexible coupling mechanism is achieved by a flange. There is a flange which is inserted into a rotary disc, circular hole. In this disc is inserted, this flange is inserted and there is a rubber padding here. So what happens during one half of the rotation, it transmits the power and drives the cross feed screw and in the other half, during the return stroke, part of the return stroke, it drives the cross feed screw and the rest of the time it remains inactive and does not transmit any power. Rather it slips over the leather padding. So here you can see the flange 4 is connected to shaft 23 in the previous slide. Here it is 5. It is connected by 2 bolts here, number 4, number 2 and 16 is this block 16. This is the feed disc which I have shown you in the previous diagram. This is the feed disc, same disc is shown here and on top of this, there is a leather side on which this block is mounted. Block 1 is mounted. This block 1 is connected to a connecting rod 15. It is connected to a connecting rod 15 which is connected to a rack 14 and rack 14 meshes with another shaft, another gear which is gear number. This is the gear on which it is meshes. This gear, it meshes, rack meshes with this gear. On this gear, it is mounted on a shaft 12. On this same shaft, gear 9 is mounted and gear 10 is mounted. 10 is running loose on this shaft and gear 9 is fixed. So if shaft 12 rotates, gear 9 rotates and it does not transmit motion to the gear 10. So in this gear 10, there is a double sided pawl is fitted. This is called double pawl. You can insert this side or this side in the gear teeth 9. In any of the gear teeth 9. Now let us see what happens. This is the forward stroke. During the forward stroke, it will rotate in this direction. So the power from here, the power is being transmitted, so this will also rotate. As this rotates by friction between the pad and this flange, the power will be transmitted to the disc and this will rotate here in this direction. So as it comes down, now here as it comes down, the connecting rod will come down, so this rack will come down. And we will fit this hand, let us say here, so this will rotate in. If this comes down, so this will rotate in this direction. And this hand is fixed here. The left hand is slid inside this here. So when it comes down, this slides and no movement is transferred to the gear 11. However, and the rest of the time, it is stopped by, after a period of rotation, it is stopped by a stopper 7, fixed pin 7. Now at the end of the return stroke, forward stroke, it returns. So the direction changes. When the direction changes, it returns in reverse direction. In reverse direction, the crank, it will move up and this rack will move up. When it moves up, the gear 9 will give a positive motion to the gear 11 through the pawl 10. As a result, this shaft 4 will move because it masses and cross feed will be given. So this is how it works. The planar feed mechanism works in a planar. So this is one of the mechanisms. Not necessary that all planar machines will be using this feed mechanism. This is one of the feed mechanism which can be implemented in a planar. So comparison of separ and planar. The planar is specially adapted for large work. Separ can do only small work. Planar, the work is moved against the stationary tool. On separ, the tool moves across the work which is stationary. On planar, tool is fed to the work and on separ, the work is fed to the tool. The drive on the planar is either by gears or by hydraulic machines. The separ ramp can also be driven in this manner. But many a times, a quick return mechanism is used. Most planar deforms of separ is that they approach a more constant cutting velocity. In planar, the tool is rigidly supported and the work moves on precision guideways. This gives much more accuracy in comparison with separ where there is a cantilever thing happens and there is overhanging happens. So accuracy is compromised and surface finish is compromised. So multiple tool can cut in a planar which is not possible in a separ machine. In planar, work setting requires skill and time whereas separ work setting is very very easy. Tool used in planar outpost will be much more robust because the work we are using is larger. Of course, the planar machine is much heavier and costlier than separ machine. So these are the some of the comparison between separ and planar which is very often asked in the examination. So you can write these comparisons and what are the different operations that can be performed on a planar machine. You can machine horizontals just like separ. All these operations that can be performed in separ machine can also be performed on a planar machine but the accuracy is high and the work piece are much larger compared to that what we do in separ machine. So this is all about slotter and planar. I hope you have liked this lecture. Thank you for watching.
